title: Periodicity of physical properties of the elements in Period 3
summary: 9701 AS Period 3 trends — atomic radius, ionisation energy, melting point, and conductivity with PhET build-an-atom.
learningObjectives: Describe trends in atomic and ionic radius across Period 3.
Explain first ionisation energy variations including anomalies.
Relate melting points to structure and bonding.

simpleExplanation.title: Patterns across Period 3
simpleExplanation.summary: Nuclear charge increases across Period 3 while electrons occupy the same shell — radius falls and ionisation energy generally rises, with jumps at Groups 13 and 16.
steps: Atomic radius ↓: greater nuclear attraction on same n shell.
IE ↑ overall — dip at Al (3p easier than 3s) and S (pair repulsion in 3p).
Melting point: peak at Si (giant covalent); metals high, S₈ molecular low.
Conductivity: metals conduct; Si semiconducts; P, S insulators.

sections.content: **Period 3 physical properties** (9.1) pair with **chemical periodicity** (9.2). Examiners expect **explanations** linked to **nuclear charge** and **structure**, not memorised lists alone.
